title: phegopteris rostrata
intro: Phegopteris rostrata, also known as the toothed polypody or rostrate polypody, is a species of fern native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Phegopteris rostrata is often used as a decoction or tea, and it can also be found in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
